destinations.evidence: The provider stores the shared verifier trait object and clones the Arc into each RitualBlockExecutor it creates.
The block executor uses the shared verifier during block execution to update the in-memory registry from on-chain executor state.

equivalence_argument: The dependency provider carries an Arc<dyn SpcVerifier> from builder construction into concrete block executors, and block execution uses that shared object rather than creating a local verifier.
rationale: The primary commit imports SpcVerifierImpl, clones ctx.spc_verifier(), wraps it, and replaces BasicBlockExecutorProvider::new with new_with_spc(..., Some(spc_verifier)), explicitly connecting the shared verifier to execution. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: When RitualExecutorBuilder creates its block executor provider, the provider must be constructed with an SPC verifier derived from BuilderContext rather than with no SPC verifier.
suggested_falsification_tests: Instantiate RitualExecutorBuilder with a BuilderContext carrying a test SPC verifier, create the executor provider, execute or simulate a block path that requires SPC verification, and assert that the shared verifier is invoked; the test should fail if BasicBlockExecutorProvider is created without Some(spc_verifier).
summary: The dependency block executor receives and uses the shared SPC verifier.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Instantiate RitualExecutorBuilder with a BuilderContext carrying a test SPC verifier, create the executor provider, execute or simulate a block path that requires SPC verification, and assert that the shared verifier is invoked; the test should fail if BasicBlockExecutorProvider is created without Some(spc_verifier).
title: Block executor uses shared SPC verifier
